text: what would she have done, without him? She’d survived on her own before they met. Ronin knew she would have carried on. Escaping the Bot District would have proven difficult, but Lara was resourceful . Metal groaned as she pulled. Her legs tightened around him. “ Almost …” Ronin exerted gradual pressure opposite hers, pushing his leg down to the bed while she tugged upward. She leaned back, her hair draping over his blackened chest-casing, and released a sound from deep in her throat that was at once a growl and a grunt . With a metallic pang, she fell backward again . “Got the bastard!” She raised the pliers, showing Ronin the jagged, deformed chunk of metal gripped between the teeth . Lara slid off him as he sat up. He drew his right foot toward his rear, slowly bending his knee. Internally, the actuator whined and vibrated, giving diminished return on the power it drew. It was still a significant improvement over the joint being locked in place . “Thank you,” he said, turning his optics to Lara . “We ain’t done yet,” she replied, tucking a mass of wild curls behind her ear. Extending her hand, she touched a finger to one of the pieces of shrapnel protruding from his chest casing. “What happened, anyway? Your upper half is torn to shreds, but your bottom half looks mostly fine .” “Found an old house out there. There were blankets and sheets hung up all over the basement .” She tugged one of the pieces from his chest. His sensors registered a tiny flare of pain, a ghostly echo of what he’d suffered in that basement. It wasn’t nearly as haunting as the corpses . “Keep talking,” she said, moving the pliers to the next bit . “By sheer chance, I missed the tripwire when I worked my way to the back. There was a workbench there. Picked up the reloading press and some cans — ” Lara’s hand jerked back, pulling another piece of shrapnel. She dropped it into his lap . “—and decided it was time to head back,” he continued as she removed more. “I’d already searched the other rooms. As I pushed through the hangings, something caught on my leg. Heard the device arm, but I wasn’t fast enough. The people who lived there rigged up an explosive, probably their final line of defense, and I triggered it .” “I’m glad it wasn’t worse.” She grunted, rocking the pliers to loosen one of the larger pieces . “The sheets caught fire and enveloped me. I tripped over my pack, which is probably what saved me from further damage. It kicked up a cloud of dust that put out the flames.” He glanced down at his arms and brushed the last bits of his ruined coat’s sleeves onto the floor . “All right. Only one left, as far as I can tell.” She settled her eyes on his damaged optic. “Do you…want me to try to get it out ?” “No,” he replied. “The risk of further damage to my optical system is too high .” “What else can I do , then ?” Ronin hesitated. She’d called it morbid, but it was a necessity, and it would earn a little credit in the Market. “Need to get the damaged skin off. I had to disable the entire interface due to shorted sensors .” Her eyes widened, falling to his abdomen, where most of the blackened, melted skin clung . Chapter Twenty - One L ara stared at Ronin’s ruined flesh. Pulling the pieces of metal out of his casing had been like plucking out splinters, and she’d done that more times than she could count for herself and Tabitha. But this … This was skin ! Even if it wasn’t living flesh, it was a part of Ronin…and he wanted her to cut it off. She bit her lower lip, looking up to meet his steady, one- eyed gaze . “Does it hurt?” she asked . “No. Not anymore.” He turned and swung his legs over the side of the bed. “I can take care of it .” She placed a hand on his chest. He froze. It was eerie, how suddenly and completely his movement halted . “I’ll do it, Ronin. Just…tell me what I need to do.” Her hand fell away from his chest . Slowly, he turned his head, eye dipping to follow her hand. “You’ve done enough already .” “God dammit, Ronin!” She sprung off the bed and threw her arms to the side. “I said I’d do it. It’s the least I can do after everything you’ve done for me .” Lara collected the tools from the chest, unsure of which she’d need, and spread them atop the bed . “We’ll get your back first .” He was quiet for a long while, his lip-plates pressed together. Just as she wondered if he’d shut down, he twisted and picked up a knife, staring down at it . “Use this to cut around my waist. An inch or two below the scorch marks.” He deftly spun the knife on his palm, holding the grip to her . “Okay.” She wrapped her fingers around the handle. The knife was impossibly heavy . Ronin turned away and stood, hooking his thumbs on the waist of his pants to tug them down below the burn marks. While his front had been devastated, his back was damaged mainly near his shoulders and at his sides . Sitting on the edge of the bed, Lara raised the knife, stilling the blade just before it touched his skin. Her stomach churned. She’d never cut anyone, apart from the accident with Tabitha. Now she was about to cut into the only other person she cared about . He said it doesn’t hurt . She had to trust his word. Inhaling deeply, she pressed the blade to his skin.
